DOWNHILL REPEATS: After the main set, find a moderate gradient hill. 6x2min controlled fast downhill (same hill, reverse direction). Focus on short quick steps, slight forward lean, relax quads. Walk back up as recovery. This is CRITICAL for Balcons descents — eccentric quad strength is what separates finishers from sufferers in the final 20km.

runMeta.why: Develops VO2max and neuromuscular efficiency at high intensities. Even for ultra runners, periodic high-intensity work improves top-end fitness that translates to faster sustainable pace at lower efforts.

WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Maintain upper body and core endurance; focus on recovery mobility before race-specific phase.

WARM-UP (5 min): Arm circles x15 each, Cat-cow x10, Thoracic rotations x10 each

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Push-ups — 3x15 — Rest 60s
A2. Bent-over Rows — 3x12 — Rest 60s
B1. Shoulder Press — 3x10 — Rest 60s
B2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 3x12 each — Rest 45s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x15 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Side Plank 45s each | Pallof Press 12 each | Russian Twists 15 each

MOBILITY (10 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Thoracic extension 2 min
FULL REST: Save energy for Saturday's big effort.

Details: Big effort - practice race day nutrition
WorkoutDescription: PEAK LONG RUN: 3h30 - biggest run of base phase. Practice EVERYTHING: race nutrition (60-80g carbs/hr), hydration, pacing, gear. This is a dress rehearsal. Stay in Z2 but expect fatigue in final hour.

blocks.name: Steady Long Run
blocks.description: Easy aerobic pace, walk uphills if needed

runMeta.why: Builds mitochondrial density and fat oxidation — the aerobic engine for ultra running. Z2 specifically develops your ability to sustain effort for hours without glycogen depletion. At this stage of base build, the goal is time-on-feet adaptation, not speed.

WorkoutDescription: BACK-TO-BACK: Running easy on fatigued legs from yesterday's long run. Keep HR Z1-Z2 strictly. 1h30 on trails at conversational pace. This teaches your body to run efficiently when glycogen-depleted — the key skill for 100km+ racing. Take in 30-40g carbs/h to practice fueling on tired legs.

blocks.name: Steady Long Run
blocks.description: Easy aerobic pace, walk uphills if needed

runMeta.nutrition: 30-40g carbs/h to practice fueling on tired legs
runMeta.why: Bridges the gap between easy runs and long runs. Builds aerobic endurance at moderate duration, preparing your body for the progressively longer efforts ahead.

WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Maintain peak strength without adding fatigue — bridge session into race-specific block.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 3x8 each leg (same weight as W9) — Rest 75s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 3x8 (same weight as W9) — Rest 90s
B1. Step-downs (2s eccentric) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Hip Thrust — 3x10 — Rest 60s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x12 each — Rest 45s
C2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x6 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Dead Bugs 15 reps | Side Plank 40s each

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY RUN: Standard Z2 aerobic run.

runMeta.why: Active recovery and aerobic maintenance. Keeps blood flowing to repair muscle damage from harder sessions while adding low-stress volume. Critical for absorbing the training load of base build.

WorkoutDescription: EASY + STRIDES: Light session. 45min easy + 4x20s strides to stay sharp.

blocks.name: Strides
blocks.description: Gradual acceleration to 90%, walk back recovery

runMeta.why: Active recovery and aerobic maintenance. Keeps blood flowing to repair muscle damage from harder sessions while adding low-stress volume. Critical for absorbing the training load of base build. The strides maintain neuromuscular speed and running economy without adding fatigue.

WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Reduce training stress while keeping muscles engaged — transition to race-specific preparation.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK (reduced):
A1. Single-leg Deadlift (light) — 2x10 each — Rest 45s
A2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 2x12 each — Rest 45s
B1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 2x15 each — Rest 30s

CORE CIRCUIT (x2, 45s rest):
Plank 50s | Dead Bugs 12 reps | Pallof Press 10 each

MOBILITY (15 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, Pigeon pose 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Thoracic foam roll 2 min, Hamstring stretch 60s each
FULL REST: Prepare for race simulation.

Details: Race simulation - full kit / nutrition / pacing
WorkoutDescription: EXTENDED RACE SIMULATION: 4h30-5h with race kit, poles (if using), and full nutrition protocol. This is your confidence builder before Balcons. Treat the first 3h as easy, final 1h30 at race effort. Practice aid station routine: what you grab, how long you stop. Aim for 60g carbs/h. Wear exact race shoes and pack.

runMeta.keyFocus: Race simulation — full kit, nutrition, pacing
runMeta.why: Full dress rehearsal for race day. Tests nutrition strategy, pacing discipline, gear, and mental resilience over race-like duration. Identifies problems before they happen on race day.

WorkoutDescription: RECOVERY: End of Phase 1. Congratulations on completing base build! Easy recovery today.

runMeta.why: Active recovery to promote blood flow and repair. Very easy effort — this is about healing, not training. Walking is perfectly acceptable if legs feel heavy.

Details: Eccentric squats / step-downs / Nordic curls / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Build eccentric quad and hamstring strength specifically to protect knees on Balcons' long descents.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, Bodyweight lunges x10

MAIN BLOCK — ECCENTRIC FOCUS:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 4x8 each @4010 tempo (4s down, 1s up) — Rest 90s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 3x8 @3010 tempo (3s down) — Rest 90s
B1. Step-downs (slow 4s lowering) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x6 (slow eccentric) — Rest 60s
C1. Walking Lunges — 3x8 each @3010 tempo (3s down) — Rest 60s
C2. Single-leg Calf Raise (3s eccentric) — 3x12 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Pallof Press 12 each | Dead Bugs 15 reps

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY + STRIDES: 45min Z2 on trails + 5x20s strides. Get comfortable on technical terrain.

blocks.name: Strides
blocks.description: Gradual acceleration to 90%, walk back recovery

runMeta.why: Aerobic maintenance between harder Balcons-specific sessions. Easy running builds capillary density and aids recovery while keeping weekly volume on track. The strides maintain neuromuscular speed and running economy without adding fatigue.

WorkoutDescription: RACE PACE WORK: 4x8min at expected race effort (Z3-Z4). This is harder than tempo - closer to what you'll sustain in the race. 3min jog between. Learn what race pace feels like.

runMeta.why: Dials in your target effort for the Balcons 68k. Practising race pace teaches your body and brain what sustainable feels like — so you don't go out too fast on race day.

WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Single-leg stability and balance to prevent ankle rolls and maintain form on technical Balcons terrain.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Single-leg balance 30s each

MAIN BLOCK — STABILITY FOCUS:
A1. Single-leg Deadlift — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
A2. Single-leg Squat (to box) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B1. Step-downs (controlled) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 3x10 each — Rest 45s
C1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 3x12 each — Rest 45s
C2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x5 — Rest 60s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Side Plank 40s each | Pallof Press 12 each | Dead Bugs 15 reps

MOBILITY (8 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
REST OR EASY: Rest preferred.

runMeta.why: Recovery day — full rest or very light movement. Your body adapts during rest, not during training. Don't waste recovery opportunities.

WorkoutDescription: COURSE SIMULATION: 3h30 with 1500m+ D+. If possible, run similar terrain to Balcons d'Azur. Practice race nutrition and pacing strategy.

blocks.name: Steady Long Run
blocks.description: Easy aerobic pace, walk uphills if needed

runMeta.why: Race-specific long run targeting the Balcons' climbing demands. Combines time-on-feet with significant vertical to prepare your legs for sustained ascent and descent over 68k.

WorkoutDescription: BACK-TO-BACK for Balcons d'Azur prep: 1h30 easy on fatigued legs from yesterday's long run. Keep HR Z1-Z2 strictly. Running on glycogen-depleted legs is the most race-specific thing you can do for a 68k. Practice your race nutrition strategy.

blocks.name: Steady Long Run
blocks.description: Easy aerobic pace, walk uphills if needed

runMeta.why: Mid-week volume that supports Balcons preparation. Longer than easy runs but shorter than the weekend long run — builds cumulative fatigue resistance.

Details: Maintenance: same load as W11 / eccentric / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Maintain eccentric strength gains without adding new stimulus — hold steady approaching race day.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, A-skips x20

MAIN BLOCK — MAINTAIN (same weight as W11):
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 3x8 each @4010 tempo — Rest 90s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 3x8 @3010 tempo — Rest 90s
B1. Step-downs (4s lowering) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x6 — Rest 60s
C1. Walking Lunges — 3x8 each @3010 tempo — Rest 60s
C2. Single-leg Calf Raise (3s eccentric) — 3x12 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Dead Bugs 15 reps | Pallof Press 12 each

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each
EASY RUN: Standard Z2 on trails.

runMeta.why: Aerobic maintenance between harder Balcons-specific sessions. Easy running builds capillary density and aids recovery while keeping weekly volume on track.

WorkoutDescription: HILL REPEATS: 6x3min hard uphill. Push into Z5 on final minute of each rep. This is last hard session before taper.

runMeta.why: Race-specific hill power for the Balcons' relentless climbing. These repeats build the muscular endurance to maintain form on the long ascents that define this race.

WorkoutDescription: EASY RUN: Short and easy. Recovery from Wednesday.

runMeta.why: Aerobic maintenance between harder Balcons-specific sessions. Easy running builds capillary density and aids recovery while keeping weekly volume on track.

WorkoutDescription: FULL REST: Beginning pre-race rest.

Details: Last big long run before race
WorkoutDescription: FINAL LONG RUN: Last 3h run before Balcons. Keep it easy, don't push. Practice final gear check.

blocks.name: Steady Long Run
blocks.description: Easy aerobic pace, walk uphills if needed

runMeta.why: Aerobic endurance building toward the Balcons 68k. These long runs develop the fat-burning engine and mental resilience to keep moving for 8+ hours on race day.

WorkoutDescription: RECOVERY: Easy bike or walk. Taper begins.

runMeta.why: Active recovery to promote blood flow and repair. Very easy effort — this is about healing, not training. Walking is perfectly acceptable if legs feel heavy.

WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Stay sharp without creating any muscle damage — race readiness is the priority.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK (50% weight, 2 sets only):
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 2x6 each (50% weight) — Rest 45s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 2x6 (50% weight) — Rest 60s
B1. Step-ups (bodyweight) — 2x6 each — Rest 45s
B2. Glute Bridge — 2x10 — Rest 30s
C1. Calf Raise (bodyweight) — 2x12 — Rest 30s

CORE (1 round):
Plank 40s | Dead Bugs 8 reps | Side Plank 25s each

MOBILITY (15 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, Pigeon pose 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Thoracic foam roll 2 min
TAPER RUN: Easy 40min. May feel heavy - that's normal during taper.

runMeta.why: Keeps the aerobic system ticking over without adding fatigue before Balcons d'Azur 68k. Legs should feel progressively fresher as race day approaches.

Details: Keep legs fresh with short accelerations
WorkoutDescription: SHAKEOUT: 30min easy + 4x30s strides (Z5). Strides keep neuromuscular system primed without creating fatigue.

runMeta.why: Pre-race activation to maintain neuromuscular readiness before Balcons d'Azur 68k. Just enough to keep the legs feeling sharp and responsive.

WorkoutDescription: FULL REST: Rest and prepare mentally.
SHORT EASY: 30min easy jog. Legs should start feeling fresh.

runMeta.why: Keeps the aerobic system ticking over without adding fatigue before Balcons d'Azur 68k. Legs should feel progressively fresher as race day approaches.

WorkoutDescription: SHAKEOUT: 20min easy + 3x20s strides. Keep legs activated.

runMeta.why: Pre-race activation to maintain neuromuscular readiness before Balcons d'Azur 68k. Just enough to keep the legs feeling sharp and responsive.

WorkoutDescription: FULL REST: 6 days to race. Rest, hydrate, carb-load begins.
RACE WEEK SHAKEOUT: 25min very easy. Stay loose.

runMeta.why: Light movement to stay loose before Balcons d'Azur 68k. Just enough to keep muscles supple and the nervous system primed without burning any glycogen.

WorkoutDescription: FULL REST: Focus on sleep and nutrition.
ACTIVATION: 15min easy + 3x20s strides (Z5). Brief efforts keep you sharp.

runMeta.why: Light activation before Balcons d'Azur 68k. Flushes metabolic waste, primes the nervous system, and confirms everything feels good. Short strides wake up fast-twitch fibers without adding any fatigue.

WorkoutDescription: FULL REST: Two days to race.
REST OR WALK: 15-20min walk if restless, otherwise full rest.

runMeta.why: Rest is the priority — only move if it genuinely helps you feel better. Your fitness is fully banked for Balcons d'Azur 68k; now it's about arriving at the start line fresh and ready.

WorkoutDescription: PRE-RACE REST: Prepare gear, lay out nutrition, sleep early.

Details: First ultra test - conservative start - execute nutrition plan
WorkoutDescription: RACE DAY: Balcons d'Azur 68k. Start conservative! First 20km should feel easy. Execute nutrition plan (60-80g carbs/hr from start). Walk all steep uphills. This is a training race - finish strong, learn for Gypaète.

blocks.name: Final 18km
blocks.description: Push if feeling good, stay controlled

runMeta.nutrition: 60-80g carbs/hr from start, eat early and often
runMeta.keyFocus: Training race — conservative execution, nail nutrition
runMeta.why: Your first ultra of the season — 68k with serious vertical. All the base build and hill work converges here. Execute your pacing and nutrition plan, stay disciplined in Z2 on climbs, and trust your training.

WorkoutDescription: Repos total. Étirements doux 15 min, rouleau de massage quadriceps/mollets. Compression si dispo. Hydratation ++ (3L eau). Objectif perte de poids : commencer à réduire les portions et l'alcool dès maintenant.

Details: Marche active ou vélo très doux
WorkoutDescription: Marche rapide 30 min OU 30 min vélo stationnaire Z1 (résistance minimale). Pas de course. FC strictement < 138 bpm. L'objectif est de faire circuler le sang pour accélérer la récupération sans stresser les muscles.

Details: Haut du corps + core (pas de jambes)
WorkoutDescription: SALLE — 3x12 rowing machine, 3x12 développé épaules haltères, 3x15 pompes, 3x15 face pulls (élastique), 4x45s planche frontale, 3x30s planche latérale (chaque côté), 3x20 russian twist avec 5kg. Stretching 10 min.
Premier run depuis l'UTBA. Terrain PLAT uniquement. FC strictement < 138 bpm. Si les jambes sont lourdes après 15 min, marcher le reste. Ne pas forcer. C'est un test, pas un entraînement.

SALLE — 20 min vélo ou rameur Z1 (FC < 138) + 15 min mobilité dynamique (cercles de hanches, fentes avec rotation, squat profond tenu, étirements actifs chevilles) + 15 min core : dead bugs 3x10, bird dogs 3x10, planche 3x40s.
Trail facile ~8 km / ~150m D+. FC < 148 strict. Allure confortable, capable de parler sans essoufflement. Objectif : vérifier que les jambes répondent correctement. Si OK = semaine 2 en confiance.

Jour OFF. Pesée du matin (noter dans le suivi). Planifier les sorties montagne de la semaine prochaine. Préparer les repas de la semaine (meal prep). IMPORTANT : acheter/emprunter une CEINTURE THORACIQUE (Garmin HRM-Pro, Polar H10, Coros HR) pour le test de seuil de jeudi prochain.

SALLE — Réintroduction progressive des jambes. 3x15 squats goblet (poids léger), 3x12 fentes marchées (poids du corps), 3x15 step-ups banc 40cm (poids du corps), 3x12 leg curl machine, 3x20 mollets debout, 3x8 squats excentriques (4 sec descente, poids léger). Core : 4x45s planche, 3x20 russian twist.

10 min échauffement footing plat Z1 (FC < 138), puis 5x2 min en côte pente 10-15% (effort Z3 = 153-168 bpm, régulier), récupération descente marche/jogging léger. 10 min retour calme Z1. Focus : technique de marche active en montée (petits pas, poussée bâtons si dispo).

SALLE — 25 min stairmaster progressif (5 min Z1 <138, 10 min Z2 138-153, 5 min Z3 153-168, 5 min Z2) + 3x12 rowing, 3x12 développé épaules, 3x15 face pulls, 3x15 pompes déclinées, 3x30s superman dorsal.

Details: ⚠️ TEST DE SEUIL LACTIQUE — ceinture thoracique OBLIGATOIRE
WorkoutDescription: ⚠️ SÉANCE CLÉ — CEINTURE THORACIQUE OBLIGATOIRE (Garmin HRM-Pro, Polar H10 ou similaire). Protocole : 10 min échauffement progressif (marche → jogging doux). Puis 30 min à effort 'confortablement dur' sur terrain PLAT (route ou chemin régulier, PAS de côtes) — tu dois pouvoir parler par phrases courtes mais pas tenir une conversation. Relever la FC MOYENNE des 20 DERNIÈRES MINUTES du bloc de 30 min = ton SEUIL LACTIQUE approximatif. 10 min retour calme. NOTER CETTE VALEUR et me la communiquer — les zones d'entraînement seront recalibrées si nécessaire. Les zones actuelles sont corrigées de +8 bpm par rapport aux données UTBA (capteur poignet).

SALLE — Focus excentrique (protection descentes) : 4x8 squats excentriques (4s descente, 1s montée), 3x10 fentes bulgares (pied arrière sur banc), 3x15 mollets excentriques (monter 2 pieds, descendre 1 pied, 4s), 3x12 leg curl. 15 min mobilité hanches/chevilles.

~22 km / 800m D+. Allure Z2 stricte (138-153 bpm). MARCHER toutes les montées > 15% de pente. Courir les plats et descentes douces. Bâtons si terrain le permet. Nutrition : 1 C90+sel par heure. Tester le ravitaillement en course. Si tu as la ceinture thoracique, la porter pour valider les zones du test de jeudi.

Lendemain de sortie longue : 20 min footing TRÈS souple Z1 (FC < 138) OU 30 min vélo Z1 + 20 min stretching complet (ischio-jambiers, quadriceps, mollets, hanches, dos). Objectif : récupérer activement.

SALLE — Monter en charge : 4x10 back squats (ajouter 5kg vs semaine 2), 3x12 fentes marchées avec haltères, 4x15 step-ups banc haut lestés, 4x8 squats excentriques (4s), 3x12 leg curl, 3x20 mollets assis+debout. Core : 4x50s planche, 3x15 russian twist 8kg, 3x10 dead bugs lestés.

10 min échauffement Z1 (< 138). 6x4 min en montée effort Z3-Z4 (153-178 bpm) AVEC BÂTONS (technique : planter-pousser-relâcher). Récupération descente jogging/marche. 10 min retour calme. La montée doit être > 10% de pente. Simuler les montées du Gypaète.

SALLE — 35 min stairmaster (10 min Z1 <138, 15 min Z2 138-153, 5 min Z3 153-168, 5 min Z2) + 3x12 rowing lourd, 3x12 développé épaules, 3x15 face pulls, 3x20 pompes, 4x30s planche + superman alternés.

10 min échauffement Z1. 45 min allure tempo Z2-Z3 (143-166 bpm) sur sentier vallonné (~12 km, ~350m D+). Allure de course du Gypaète (~10-11 min/km). 15 min retour calme Z1. Objectif : trouver TON allure de croisière pour la course.

SALLE — 4x10 squats excentriques (5s descente maintenant !), 3x12 fentes bulgares profondes avec haltères, 4x15 mollets excentriques, 3x12 ischio-jambiers nordiques (ou leg curl lent). 15 min mobilité complète.

~26 km / 1100m D+. Gros morceau montagne. MARCHER les montées raides, bâtons, courir plats et descentes. Nutrition COMPLÈTE (C90+sel/h + 1 barre solide toutes les 2h). Pratiquer la gestion d'effort : ne JAMAIS dépasser 158 bpm en montée.

BACK-TO-BACK : courir le lendemain de la sortie longue. ~9 km / 200m D+ en terrain vallonné. Allure très facile Z1-Z2 (FC < 148). Objectif : apprendre à courir sur jambes pré-fatiguées comme aux km 50+ du Gypaète. C'est dur mais c'est ÇA qui fait la différence.

SALLE — Circuit : 4 tours de (12 squats poids modéré + 10 box jumps 40cm + 15 step-ups + 12 fentes sautées + 45s planche), 45s repos entre exercices, 2 min repos entre tours. Puis 3x20 mollets, 3x15 leg curl. Terminer par 10 min stretching.

10 min échauffement. Trouver la plus grosse montée disponible (500-700m D+ d'un coup). La grimper en effort soutenu Z3-Z4 (153-183 bpm, marche rapide + bâtons). Chronométrer. Descendre en jogging Z1 (travailler la descente technique). Si montée < 500m, faire 2 répétitions.

SALLE — 5x4 min rameur effort seuil Z3-Z4 (153-183 bpm) / 2 min repos actif Z1. Puis core intense : 4x60s planche, 3x20 russian twist 10kg, 3x15 leg raises, 3x10 ab rollout (si roue dispo). Ce core fort = posture maintenue à H12 de course.

~8 km dont 300m D-. Chercher des descentes techniques (racines, pierres, pente > 15%). Travailler : appuis rapides, regard 3m devant, bras écartés pour l'équilibre. FC < 148 en montée (marcher si nécessaire). Les 5000m de D- du Gypaète vont être brutaux à 90+ kg. Préparer les cuisses.

SALLE — 5x5 squats lourds (charge la plus haute possible avec bonne forme), 4x6 presse jambes lourde, 4x8 squats excentriques (6s descente !), 3x20 mollets debout + assis, 3x10 ischio nordiques. DERNIÈRE séance jambes lourdes avant le taper.

~30 km / 1300m D+. LA plus grosse sortie du plan. Simuler le Gypaète : montées en marche active avec bâtons, descentes en courant, plats en trot. Nutrition identique à la course (C90+sel/h, barres, eau). Sac complet. Tester TOUT : chaussures, chaussettes, crème anti-frottement. FC Z2 (138-153) sur les plats, marcher dès que ça dépasse 158.

BACK-TO-BACK #2 : ~10 km / 300m D+ sur terrain vallonné. Jambes lourdes = normal. Allure Z1-Z2 stricte (FC < 148). Ne PAS forcer. L'objectif c'est d'habituer le corps à avancer quand tout dit stop. C'est exactement ce que tu vivras aux km 55-75 du Gypaète.

SALLE — 3x20 squats poids modéré, 3x20 fentes marchées, 3x60s wall sit, 3x20 step-ups, 4x20 mollets. Repos courts (45s). L'objectif n'est pas la force max mais la résistance musculaire sur la durée. Core : 3x50s planche, 3x15 russian twist.

10 min échauffement. 4x8 min en montée allure course Z3 (effort régulier 153-168 bpm, pas explosif) AVEC BÂTONS. Récupération descente jogging technique (travailler les appuis). 10 min retour calme. Pente > 10%. Simuler les ascensions du Gypaète.

SALLE — 50 min stairmaster non-stop (simuler 1h de montée continue : 20 min Z1 <138, 20 min Z2 138-153, 10 min Z3 153-168). Record personnel de durée sur la machine. Puis 20 min express haut du corps : rowing, épaules, pompes.

~9 km dont 400m D-. Focus descente : alterner petits pas rapides et foulée plus ample selon la pente. S'habituer à la vitesse en descente. Les quads vont chauffer — c'est le but. FC < 148 en montée (marcher si nécessaire).

SALLE — Squats légers 3x10, fentes 3x8, step-ups 3x10, mobilité complète 25 min (hanches, chevilles, épaules, thoracique). Activation sans fatigue. Préparer le corps pour la répétition générale de demain.

LA SÉANCE CLÉ DE TOUT LE PLAN. ~37 km / 1600m D+. Conditions IDENTIQUES à la course : départ tôt (5h-6h), sac complet, bâtons, matériel obligatoire, nutrition C90+sel+barres. Allure cible 10-11 min/km, FC en Z2 (138-153). Tester la gestion de l'effort sur 5h+. Si possible, faire cette sortie en altitude (>1000m). Tout noter : ce qui marche, ce qui frotte, quand la fatigue arrive.

20 min marche ou vélo très souple (FC < 138) + 25 min stretching complet et rouleau de massage. Manger beaucoup de protéines (1,8g/kg). Bien dormir. Analyser les notes de la veille : qu'est-ce qu'on ajuste pour la course ?
SALLE — Semaine de décharge. Pas de jambes lourdes. 3x10 rowing léger, 3x10 épaules léger, 3x12 pompes, 3x30s planche, 3x15 face pulls, 10 min stretching. Tu devrais te sentir reposé en sortant.
~7 km / 150m D+. Récupération active. FC < 148. Tout doit être facile. Si les jambes sont encore lourdes de S5, marcher les montées. C'est normal — l'adaptation se fait pendant le repos.
SALLE — 25 min vélo Z1 (FC < 138, résistance minimale) + 15 min mobilité : cercles de hanches, fentes profondes tenues 30s, étirement ischio debout, mobilité chevilles, ouverture thoracique.

8 min échauffement, 4x2 min côte effort Z3 (153-166 bpm, modéré, pas à fond), descente jogging. 8 min retour calme. Objectif : garder la sensation de montée sans créer de fatigue. Dose minimale efficace.
Jour OFF total. Stretching si envie. Vérifier le matériel de course : chaussures (usure ?), sac, bâtons, flasques. Commander ce qui manque.

~18 km / 700m D+. Dernière sortie longue significative avant la course. Allure FACILE Z2 (138-153 bpm). Tester le matériel FINAL (pas de nouveau matériel après cette sortie). Tout ce que tu portes samedi = ce que tu porteras le 6 juin.
Jour OFF. Pesée. Tu devrais être autour de 94-95 kg. Les 2 dernières semaines vont affûter encore 1-2 kg.
SALLE — 3x10 squats (poids réduit 40% vs S4), 3x8 fentes, 3x10 step-ups, 3x30s planche, 3x10 russian twist. Pas d'excentrique lourd. Garder le tonus. Dernière séance jambes de la préparation.

8 min échauffement. 6x(1 min allure vive Z3 153-168 / 2 min souple Z1 < 138) en terrain vallonné. 8 min retour calme. Objectif : sentir de la vitesse dans les jambes sans accumuler de fatigue. Se sentir léger.

SALLE — 15 min vélo très léger + 20 min mobilité et activation : squats profonds tenus, fentes avec rotation, bandes élastiques pour activation fessiers. Préparer la liste définitive du matériel de course.
20 min footing Z1 (< 138) + 3x1 min en côte allure modérée Z3 (153-168). 5 min retour calme + marche. Garder les sensations de montée. Dernière session de côtes.

Jour OFF. Préparer le sac de course : C90 (combien de sachets pour 14h = 14 sachets minimum), barres, sel, flasques, bâtons, matériel obligatoire (vérifier la liste officielle), vêtements (prévoir froid en altitude), lampe frontale.

15 min footing très souple Z1 (< 138) + 4x30s accélération progressive (monter en allure sans sprinter). 5 min retour calme. Objectif : se sentir explosif et léger. Les jambes doivent picoter d'envie de courir.
Jour OFF. Dernière pesée avant la course. Commencer le chargement glucidique : augmenter les portions de riz/pâtes. Boire 3L d'eau.
Juste bouger. FC < 133 bpm. Plat. 20 min et c'est tout. Le corps se régénère, chaque jour de repos rend plus fort. Chargement glycogène : pâtes/riz à chaque repas.
SALLE — Zéro charge. 10 min mobilité dynamique, 10 min stretching, 3x20s planche, 2x10 dead bugs. Rouleau de massage 5 min. C'est de l'entretien, pas du travail.
10 min footing Z1 (FC < 133) + 3x15s accélération progressive (pas sprint). 3 min marche retour. C'est la DERNIÈRE course avant le Gypaète. Se sentir léger et prêt.

Repos total. CHARGEMENT GLYCOGÈNE : 8-10g glucides/kg = 800-900g de glucides dans la journée. Riz blanc, pâtes, pain blanc, miel, confiture, jus. Boire 3L. Pas d'alcool. Pas de fibres (pas de légumes crus/légumineuses). Préparer le sac FINAL.

Repos complet. Retrait dossard (vérifier horaire). Dernier gros repas MIDI (pâtes/riz). Dîner LÉGER et TÔT (18h-19h max) : riz blanc + sauce simple. Coucher avant 21h. Réveil ~03h30. Tout doit être prêt dans le sac.

Details: ★ TRAIL DU GYPAÈTE — LES 3 LACS 76,9 km / 5000 D+
WorkoutDescription: C'EST LE JOUR J ! Départ ~05h. STRATÉGIE : MARCHER toutes les montées (bâtons, petits pas, FC < 158), COURIR les plats (allure 8-9 min/km), DESCENTES technique (petits pas, pas de frein brutal). Nutrition : 1 C90+sel CHAQUE heure + 1 barre toutes les 2h + manger SALÉ aux ravitos. Hydratation 500-700ml/h. Si le moral est bas au km 50 : c'est NORMAL, ça va revenir. Ne JAMAIS abandonner dans un creux. OBJECTIF 14h. ALLEZ CHARLES !

Tu l'as fait ! REPOS TOTAL. Manger protéines + glucides dans les 60 min post-course. Hydratation ++. Compression si dispo. Pas de course pendant 7-10 jours. Profiter de la fierté d'avoir bouclé 77 km et 5000m D+ dans les Aravis. BRAVO !

ACTIVE RECOVERY: Walk only if feeling ok.

WorkoutDescription: PENULTIMATE BIG EFFORT: 4h30 with significant vertical gain. Include a mix of runnable trails and steep climbs to simulate Nice 110K terrain. Practice your full race nutrition plan (60-80g carbs/h). Run the first 3h easy, then push to race effort for the final 1h30. This is your second-to-last big session — make it count but stay injury-free.

blocks.name: Steady Long Run
blocks.description: Easy aerobic pace, walk uphills if needed

runMeta.why: Race-specific endurance for Nice 110K — the session that most closely simulates race demands. Sustained climbing and descending on tired legs builds the specific resilience you'll need over 110 kilometres.

WorkoutDescription: PEAK BACK-TO-BACK WEEKEND for Nice 110K. Running 2h easy on legs fatigued from yesterday's 4h30 long run. This is the hardest back-to-back of the plan. Stay disciplined with HR Z1-Z2. Practice your full Nice nutrition plan. Mental rehearsal: visualize the Nice course during this run.

blocks.name: Steady Long Run
blocks.description: Easy aerobic pace, walk uphills if needed

runMeta.why: Mid-week endurance in the Nice 110K build. These medium-long runs contribute to the high weekly volume your body needs to handle 110K on race day.

Details: Last hard session: eccentric / single-leg / plyos / core
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Final high-intensity strength session — last chance to load before taper; make it count.

WARM-UP (6 min): Leg swings x15 each, Hip circles x10 each, Glute bridges x15, A-skips x20

MAIN BLOCK:
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 4x8 each @4010 tempo — Rest 90s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 4x8 — Rest 90s
B1. Step-downs (4s eccentric) — 3x8 each leg — Rest 60s
B2. Nordic Hamstring Curl — 3x8 — Rest 60s
C1. Box Jumps (power) — 3x6 — Rest 90s
C2. Single-leg Calf Raise (3s eccentric) — 3x15 each — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x3, 45s rest between rounds):
Plank 60s | Pallof Press 12 each | Dead Bugs 15 reps

COOL-DOWN (5 min): Hip flexor stretch 60s each, Pigeon pose 60s each
EASY + STRIDES: 50min Z2 + 6x20s strides.

blocks.name: Strides
blocks.description: Gradual acceleration to 90%, walk back recovery

runMeta.why: Aerobic maintenance in the Nice 110K build phase. These easy sessions let you absorb the big volume weeks while keeping your running economy sharp. The strides maintain neuromuscular speed and running economy without adding fatigue.

WorkoutDescription: RACE PACE: 35min at Nice race effort. Learn what sub-18h pace feels like. This is ~6km/h average.

runMeta.why: Race-specific pace work for Nice 110K. Ingrains the rhythm and effort level you'll sustain for 15+ hours. Mental rehearsal is as important as physical here.

Details: Reduced load: light single-leg / core / mobility
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Begin volume reduction — keep muscles engaged but start dropping fatigue for race week.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK (reduced):
A1. Single-leg Deadlift (light) — 2x8 each — Rest 45s
A2. Single-leg Glute Bridge — 2x10 each — Rest 45s
B1. Single-leg Calf Raise — 2x12 each — Rest 30s
B2. Nordic Hamstring Curl (eccentric only) — 2x4 — Rest 45s

CORE CIRCUIT (x2, 45s rest):
Plank 45s | Dead Bugs 10 reps | Side Plank 30s each

MOBILITY (12 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, Pigeon pose 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Thoracic foam roll 2 min
FULL REST: Save everything for Saturday.

Details: Last big effort - full race nutrition practice
WorkoutDescription: PEAK LONG RUN — FINAL RACE SIM: 5h with full Nice 110K simulation. Race kit, nutrition every 45min (aim for 60-80g carbs/h), poles. Include a night section (start at dusk or run final 1h with headlamp). Practice headlamp management, eating in the dark, navigating with reduced visibility. This is the most important training session of the entire plan. After this, trust the work is done.

runMeta.keyFocus: Race simulation — full kit, nutrition, pacing + night running
runMeta.why: The final dress rehearsal before Nice 110K. Tests every element — nutrition timing, gear, pacing discipline, and mental resilience over race-like duration. Your last chance to identify and fix problems before the real thing.

WorkoutDescription: RECOVERY: Very easy. Taper begins now.

runMeta.why: Active recovery to promote blood flow and repair. Very easy effort — this is about healing, not training. Walking is perfectly acceptable if legs feel heavy.

WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Taper week — maintain movement patterns at 50% load to stay sharp without accumulating fatigue.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x10

MAIN BLOCK (50% weight, 2 sets):
A1. Bulgarian Split Squat — 2x6 each (50% weight) — Rest 45s
A2. Romanian Deadlift — 2x6 (50% weight) — Rest 60s
B1. Step-ups (bodyweight) — 2x6 each — Rest 45s
B2. Glute Bridge — 2x10 — Rest 30s
C1. Calf Raise (bodyweight) — 2x12 — Rest 30s

CORE (1 round):
Plank 40s | Dead Bugs 8 reps | Side Plank 25s each

MOBILITY (15 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, Pigeon pose 90s each, 90/90 stretch 60s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Thoracic foam roll 2 min
TAPER RUN: Easy 45min. May feel sluggish - normal.

runMeta.why: Keeps the aerobic system ticking over without adding fatigue before Nice 110K. Legs should feel progressively fresher as race day approaches.

WorkoutDescription: SHARPENING: 20min tempo + 4x20s strides (Z5). Keep systems primed.

runMeta.why: Threshold maintenance in the Nice build. Even at ultra distances, a higher lactate threshold means you can sustain a faster comfortable pace for longer — critical over 110K.

WorkoutDescription: FULL REST.
EASY: 40min easy.

runMeta.why: Keeps the aerobic system ticking over without adding fatigue before Nice 110K. Legs should feel progressively fresher as race day approaches.

WorkoutDescription: FINAL 2H RUN: Last longer effort. Keep it easy.

blocks.name: Steady Long Run
blocks.description: Easy aerobic pace, walk uphills if needed

runMeta.why: Peak aerobic endurance for Nice 110K. These are the sessions that build the deep fatigue resistance you'll need to keep running through the night over 110 kilometres.

WorkoutDescription: RECOVERY: Very easy.

runMeta.why: Gentle movement to promote blood flow and recovery. Keeps the body loose and the mind calm as you approach Nice 110K.

Details: Taper: very light activation / mobility only
WorkoutDescription:
Ultra Goal: Race week — minimal activation to keep neuromuscular pathways firing without any tissue stress.

WARM-UP (5 min): Leg swings x10 each, Hip circles x10 each, Bodyweight squats x8

MAIN BLOCK (activation only):
A1. Bodyweight Bulgarian Split Squat — 1x6 each — Rest 30s
A2. Bodyweight Walking Lunges — 1x6 each — Rest 30s
B1. Glute Bridge — 1x10 — Rest 30s
B2. Calf Raise (bodyweight) — 1x12 — Rest 30s

CORE (1 round):
Plank 30s | Dead Bugs 6 reps

MOBILITY (15 min): Hip flexor stretch 90s each, Pigeon pose 90s each, 90/90 stretch 90s each, Hamstring stretch 60s each, Ankle dorsiflexion 60s each, Thoracic foam roll 2 min, Quad foam roll 2 min
EASY: 35min easy.

runMeta.why: Keeps the aerobic system ticking over without adding fatigue before Nice 110K. Legs should feel progressively fresher as race day approaches.

WorkoutDescription: SHAKEOUT: 25min easy + 4x30s strides (Z5).

runMeta.why: Pre-race activation to maintain neuromuscular readiness before Nice 110K. Just enough to keep the legs feeling sharp and responsive.

WorkoutDescription: FULL REST.
EASY: Short easy jog. Legs should feel fresh.

runMeta.why: Keeps the aerobic system ticking over without adding fatigue before Nice 110K. Legs should feel progressively fresher as race day approaches.

WorkoutDescription: SHAKEOUT: 20min easy + 3x20s strides.

runMeta.why: Pre-race activation to maintain neuromuscular readiness before Nice 110K. Just enough to keep the legs feeling sharp and responsive.

WorkoutDescription: FULL REST: Race week begins.
RACE WEEK: 20min very easy shakeout.

runMeta.why: Light movement to stay loose before Nice 110K. Just enough to keep muscles supple and the nervous system primed without burning any glycogen.

WorkoutDescription: FULL REST: Focus on sleep, nutrition, hydration.
ACTIVATION: 15min easy + 3x20s strides (Z5). Brief sharp efforts.

runMeta.why: Light activation before Nice 110K. Flushes metabolic waste, primes the nervous system, and confirms everything feels good. Short strides wake up fast-twitch fibers without adding any fatigue.

WorkoutDescription: FULL REST: Carb loading. Hydrate well.
REST OR WALK: Light walk if restless.

runMeta.why: Rest is the priority — only move if it genuinely helps you feel better. Your fitness is fully banked for Nice 110K; now it's about arriving at the start line fresh and ready.

WorkoutDescription: PRE-RACE: Prepare all gear. Lay out nutrition. Sleep early. Trust your training.

Details: GOAL RACE - Execute plan - sub-18h - trust your training!
WorkoutDescription: GOAL RACE: Nice 110K. Sub-18h target (~6km/h avg). START CONSERVATIVE! First 30km should feel easy. Execute nutrition plan from kilometer 0 (60-80g carbs/hr). Walk all steep uphills. Stay calm through low points - they pass. You've done the work. TRUST YOUR TRAINING!

blocks.name: Mid Race (30-70km)
blocks.description: Settle, execute nutrition, stay calm through lows

blocks.name: Night Section (70-90km)
blocks.description: Headlamp on, stay focused, manage sleep deprivation

runMeta.nutrition: 60-80g carbs/hr from km 0, switch to gels/liquids after 12h
runMeta.keyFocus: GOAL RACE — sub-18h, conservative start, execute the plan
runMeta.why: The A-race — 110K through the night. Everything in this 9-month plan built toward this moment. Trust the aerobic base, the hill strength, the nutrition strategy. You've done the work. Execute patiently and enjoy the journey.
